title: Alpaca Nanobodies Rapid Ag test
description: {"blocks":[{"key":"fhc78","text":"","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]},{"key":"efolq","text":"The \"Rapid Ag test based on Alpaca Nanobodies\" is a test developed with single domain antibodies. This type of antibody is capable of effectively recognize the Spike protein on the surface of SARS-CoV2 leading a signal that is 10 times stronger than conventional antibodies. ","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]},{"key":"n4os","text":"","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]},{"key":"8jrb4","text":"It takes only 8 minutes from sample to result. The procedures for using this test were designed to ensure an easy use and interpretation, making it a quick and simple test.","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]}],"entityMap":{}}
before_start: {"blocks":[{"key":"7nmi1","text":"Please follow this protocol in order to use \"The Alpaca Nanobodies Rapid Ag test\" correctly and get the accurate results","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]}],"entityMap":{}}
warning: {"blocks":[{"key":"aj4qe","text":"Processing of any sample type which could potentially be positive for SARS-CoV-2 should be conducted in BSL2+ settings. Before starting work with these samples, please contact your local EHS (environment, health and safety) or biosafety office for proper guidance on how to work with these samples","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]}],"entityMap":{}}
materials_text: {"blocks":[{"key":"4e7rj","text":"Equipment List","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[{"style":"bold","offset":0,"length":14}],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]},{"key":"661im","text":" ","type":"atomic","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[{"key":0,"offset":0,"length":1}],"data":[]},{"key":"b1sc0","text":"","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]},{"key":"3spfk","text":" ","type":"atomic","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[{"key":1,"offset":0,"length":1}],"data":[]},{"key":"b6h8l","text":"","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]},{"key":"9sri6","text":"","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]},{"key":"7k08t","text":"","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]}],"entityMap":{"0":{"type":"tables","mutability":"","data":{"cellsMeta":{"0_0":"_dt-bold _dt-h-center","0_1":"_dt-bold _dt-h-center","0_2":"_dt-bold _dt-h-center","1_0":"","1_1":"","1_2":"","2_0":"","2_1":"","2_2":"","3_0":"","3_1":"","3_2":""},"colWidths":[208,153,155],"data":[["Equipment","Supplier","Catalog #"],["NucleoVac 24 Vacuum Manifold","Macherey-Nagel","740299"],["Manifold for 24 samples","KNF","046209/049540"],["Micropipette","Gilson","FA10004P"]],"guid":"C08BC1C0F1E911EAB2D487F1F73B2E31","legend":"","mergeCells":[],"rowHeights":[23,23,23,23],"show_headers":true}},"1":{"type":"tables","mutability":"","data":{"cellsMeta":{"0_0":"_dt-bold","1_0":"","2_0":"","3_0":"","4_0":"","5_0":"","6_0":""},"colWidths":[205],"data":[["Kit Content"],["Sample collection tube"],["Solution 1. \"Binding Solution\""],["Detection Column"],["Solution 2. \"Washing Buffer\""],["Solution 3. \"Detection Reagent\""],["Reference paper color"]],"guid":"CB6B9C90F1EA11EAB2D487F1F73B2E31","legend":"","mergeCells":[],"rowHeights":[23,23,23,23,23,23,23],"show_headers":true}}}}
steps: Collect saliva in the sample collection tube.
Add Solution 1 containing the saliva to the "Detection column"
Connect the column to the vacuum manifold and let the full content pass through the "Detection columns"
Mix saliva sample with 10mL of Solution 1 “Binding Solution” and mix gently for 2 minutes.
Add 100 µL of Solution 3 "Detection Reagent" and analyze after 2 minutes: blue color indicates antigen detection. Paper colour controls will be provided.
creator: Berking Biotechnology
